opp.name: Port Authority NSW_S2P_2026
opp.next_step: Schedule an onsite high-level demo at Guy Elliot’s Sydney office with key internal stakeholders. Focus the demo on Intake Management and S2C as a low-integration starting point to show quick procurement value, reduce manual processes, and address concerns around waiting for ERP implementation first.
opp.ais_why: Interested in Agentic AI as they are using Legacy tools

items.quote: No EB, CFO, or executive sponsor mapped. Guy Elliott (Senior Manager Procurement) is the sole contact; he does not control budget approval for a $200k investment.

sources: NextStep field (address concerns around waiting for ERP implementation first, reduce manual processes, show quick procurement value) — current as of 1 Jul 2026
metrics.narrative: Pain identified: buyer expressed concern about manual procurement processes and the cost/delay of waiting for ERP implementation to act on procurement transformation (source: NextStep field, current as of 1 Jul 2026). Zycus is positioning Intake Management and S2C as a low-integration starting point to deliver quick procurement value and reduce manual work. However, no quantified business case has been articulated yet—no savings target, headcount impact, cycle-time reduction, or ROI stated. The qualitative pain (manual processes, ERP-dependency concern) is clear, but moving to commercials will require quantifying the value case. Recommend: co-develop a value model with Guy Elliott ahead of the onsite demo to anchor the business case in Port Authority's procurement volume, cycle time, and manual-touch cost.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Guy Elliott (Evaluator) is the only buyer-side role
NextStep field indicates Guy Elliott owns the demo scheduling and is engaged (recent email 24 Jun 2026 per engagement pulse)
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Guy Elliott (Senior Manager Procurement) is the sole buyer-side contact and the deal's primary thread. He articulated the pain (manual processes, ERP-dependency concern) and agreed to the onsite demo as the next step, indicating engagement and interest. However, there is no evidence of advocacy, access to power, or internal selling on Zycus's behalf. A champion at this stage would introduce Zycus to the CFO or COO, share internal priorities or competitive intel, or push back on deferring the decision. Guy Elliott has done none of these yet; he is an engaged evaluator, not a coach or advocate. The deal is 34 days old, so a champion may still be developing, but right now it is single-threaded to a mid-level contact with no exec access. Recommend: during the demo planning and delivery, assess Guy Elliott's willingness to champion Zycus internally—ask him to invite the CFO, COO, or IT lead to the demo, and gauge his response. If he resists or defers, he is not a champion; coach him or find another internal ally.

sources: No Avoma calls, tasks, or NextStep mentions of competitors
competition.narrative: Competition unknown. No competitor named in any Salesforce field (Competitors__c is blank per ground truth), and no Avoma calls or tasks surfaced competitive intel. It is unclear whether Port Authority is running a multi-vendor evaluation, has an incumbent procurement system being replaced, or is sole-sourcing to the relevant stakeholder public-sector context (NSW government entity) and the product scope (full S2P suite: iContract, iSource, Merlin, iCompliance, ANA) suggest this is a significant procurement that would typically attract competitive bids (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Ivalua, Jaggaer are common in ANZ public sector). The absence of competitive intelligence is a blind spot: if a competitor is running a parallel eval and we are unaware, we cannot position differentiation or counter their strengths. Recommend: during the onsite demo planning conversation, ask Guy Elliott directly whether Port Authority is evaluating other vendors or if this is a sole-source engagement, and what (if any) incumbent system Zycus would replace.

sources: NextStep field: address concerns around waiting for ERP implementation first, reduce manual processes, show quick procurement value (current as of 1 Jul 2026)
Guy Elliott is the named contact and pain articulator (Senior Manager Procurement, OpportunityContactRole)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain identified and owned by Guy Elliott (Senior Manager Procurement). The buyer is concerned that waiting for ERP implementation before addressing procurement transformation will delay value and perpetuate manual processes (source: NextStep field, current as of 1 Jul 2026). This is operational pain (manual procurement work, process inefficiency) tied to a strategic blocker (ERP-first sequencing risk). Zycus is positioning Intake Management and S2C as a low-integration solution that can deliver quick wins without ERP dependency, directly addressing the stated pain. The pain is confirmed and prospect-articulated, but not yet quantified (no cost of manual work, no cycle-time baseline, no error-rate data). The pain owner is Guy Elliott; whether senior leadership (CFO, COO, CIO) shares this urgency is unknown. Recommend: validate the pain's executive visibility—does the CFO or COO recognize the cost of deferring procurement transformation, or is this Guy Elliott's operational concern only?

sources: No evidence in NextStep, tasks, or fields
Inferred: public-sector buyer implies formal paper process, but not yet discussed
paper_process.narrative: Paper process unknown. No discussion of MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec review, or procurement channel (direct vs panel contract). Public-sector deals in Australia often require vendor panel registration, security clearance, and board or executive sign-off; none of this has been surfaced yet. No SI or partner involvement documented (though the note flags this as partner-led APAC, the partner identity and role are not stated). No current-contract expiration or forcing function identified. The deal is too early (Qualified, 34 days old) for contracting mechanics to be front-of-mind, but InfoSec and procurement-channel fit should be qualified before investing in a full demo and proposal. Recommend: confirm during demo planning whether Zycus needs to be on a vendor panel, whether InfoSec/security review is required, and what the contract approval chain looks like.

sources: OpportunityContactRole query: only Guy Elliott (Evaluator) mapped; no EB/Executive Sponsor role present
Account contacts (prefetch): CFO Kristie Langley-Gliddon and COO Philip Holliday exist but not confirmed involved in this opp
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer not identified. No Contact Role for EB, Executive Sponsor, or CFO-level authority. Account-level contacts include Kristie Langley-Gliddon (CFO) and Philip Holliday (COO), but neither is confirmed as engaged in this opportunity's scope. A $200k S2P suite procurement in a public-sector entity will require CFO or senior executive budget approval; Guy Elliott (Senior Manager Procurement) does not control commercial sign-off. The deal cannot progress to pricing, ROI workshop, or contracting without mapping and engaging the budget holder. Recommend: ask Guy Elliott directly who approves a $200k procurement investment and request an introduction ahead of or during the onsite demo to validate executive sponsorship.

sources: No field or call evidence of decision process
Inferred: public-sector buyer (Port Authority of NSW) implies formal procurement governance, but not yet documented
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is unknown. No approval chain, procurement-governance steps, or timeline milestones have been documented. The deal is only 34 days old and in early Qualified stage, so a formal process may not yet be defined by the buyer. However, Port Authority is a public-sector entity (NSW government); expect a structured procurement process (RFx, panel review, board or executive approval for capital spend). Guy Elliott has not articulated the process. The onsite demo is the immediate next step (awaiting scheduling), but what follows—RFI, RFP, shortlist, board sign-off—is unclear. Recommend: during the demo planning conversation, ask Guy Elliott to outline Port Authority's procurement approval steps for a $200k software investment and map the timeline from demo to decision to contract signature.

sources: NextStep field: focus demo on Intake Management and S2C as low-integration starting point, quick value, reduce manual processes
decision_criteria.narrative: Product scope is defined and broad: iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, Merlin suite (Merlin Intake, Agentic AI), iCompliance, ANA (source: Products__c). The NextStep field indicates the buyer's immediate evaluation lens: low-integration fit (can it run standalone without waiting for ERP), quick procurement value (speed to ROI), and manual-process reduction (source: NextStep, 1 Jul 2026). These are preliminary fitment criteria, not a formal RFP rubric. No structured use-case requirements, vendor scorecard, or BRD has been shared. The onsite demo is the next gate to surface deeper decision criteria—functional fit for Intake Management and S2C, AI/Merlin appetite, compliance needs, integration roadmap post-ERP. Recommend: ahead of the demo, request Port Authority's high-level procurement-transformation goals or a scoping doc to tailor the demo to their decision lens.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 0: no break-risk was observed in the swept evidence. Coverage is thin, so read this as 'nothing seen yet,' not 'nothing there.'
commentary.win_position: Win 30 is carried by momentum 82 vs stage-expected 50; differentiation strength +0.85 (weight 20) — from Next-Step/narrative: 'manual process' in…. Offsetting it: preference strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 82 (moving forward): [Clari - Email Sent] Re: Onsite Demo for S2C Solution at Port Authority (depth 6.0, 6 in 30d). Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 5 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment 53 reflects real customer investment: Customer owns 3 open commitment(s); Security/procurement review underway.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 34 is anchored on the stage win (30), nudged by commitment 53 and momentum 82, and dragged by risk 0.

items.detail: Buyer concern about waiting for ERP implementation before acting on procurement may delay or defer the decision. If Port Authority decides to complete ERP first, this deal could slip indefinitely or die. Zycus is positioning low-integration quick-win to counter this, but the vulnerability is unresolved until the buyer commits to moving forward independently of the ERP project.

No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified; single-threaded to mid-level procurement manager (Guy Elliott). Without executive sponsorship or budget-holder engagement, the deal cannot progress to commercials or contracting. Vulnerability is that Guy Elliott lacks authority to commit, and we have no visibility into whether senior leadership supports this procurement investment.

Single-threaded risk: if Guy Elliott deprioritizes, leaves, or is overruled, the deal dies. No multi-threading to senior stakeholders, IT lead, or finance sponsor. A deal this size ($200k S2P suite) requires multiple advocates and executive alignment; relying on one mid-level contact is a structural vulnerability.

No competitive intelligence: do not know if Port Authority is evaluating other vendors or if this is sole-source. If a competitor is running a parallel eval and we are unaware, we cannot position our differentiation or counter their strengths. Blind to the competitive landscape.

flags: Multi-thread urgently: Only 1 buyer contact (Guy Elliott, mid-level Procurement lead) on a $200k S2P deal. Expand to senior stakeholders (Philip Holliday COO, Kellie French Corporate Services Manager, Ian Chilton IT lead) on the demo call to uncover Economic Buyer, validate urgency, and reduce single-thread risk. A deal this size cannot close through one Evaluator.

Convert the demo into a discovery + validation session: Use the onsite demo to surface quantified pain (current procurement cycle time, manual-process cost, error rate), map the decision process (RFI/RFP timeline, approval gates), identify competitors, and validate the low-integration/quick-value pitch with real buyer priorities. A demo without discovery leaves the MEDDPICC gaps unfilled.
No Economic Buyer or Decision Maker identified across Contact Roles, Salesforce fields, or any Avoma calls. Cannot progress to commercials or contracting without budget authority engaged.
No Champion identified. Guy Elliott shows no evidence of advocate behavior (no EB/DM introductions, no internal selling, no coaching). Champion strength is weak.
No decision criteria, process, or approval-chain visibility. Do not know how buyer will evaluate vendors or what gates must be passed to close.

Unresolved deferral risk: buyer concern about waiting for ERP implementation before procurement tooling. If buyer decides ERP-first, deal slips indefinitely or dies. Zycus positioning (low-integration quick win) is response, not resolution.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Buyer's immediate expectation: a low-integration procurement solution that delivers quick value without waiting for ERP implementation, reduces manual processes, and demonstrates ROI fast. Zycus's positioning (Intake Management + S2C as a standalone starting point) is aligned with this expectation. Product scope is broad (iContract, iSource, Merlin suite, iCompliance, ANA), suggesting Port Authority has or will articulate a full S2P vision; the low-integration entry is the wedge to prove value before expanding. No formal RFP or BRD yet, so deeper functional fit (AI appetite, compliance requirements, integration roadmap post-ERP) is unconfirmed. The onsite demo is the gate to validate fit.

A $200k S2P suite deal in a public-sector entity requires CFO (Kristie Langley-Gliddon), COO (Philip Holliday), or CIO (Garry Voutos) engagement to validate budget, strategic fit, and executive sponsorship. Ask Guy Elliott to invite at least one exec to the demo and use the demo as a forcing function to widen the stakeholder base.

deltas.to: Surface competitive reality during demo planning. Ask Guy Elliott directly whether Port Authority is evaluating other vendors or if this is sole-source, and wha
deltas.subject: Surface competitive reality during demo planning. Ask Guy Elliott directly whether Port Authority is evaluating other vendors or if this is sole-source, and what (if any) incumbent system Zycus would replace. If competitors are present, tailor the demo to position Zycus's AI/Merlin differentiation and low-integration entry advantage; if sole-source, lean into speed-to-value and bypass-ERP positioning to lock in the eval before competition emerges.

deltas.to: Co-develop a quantified value case ahead of or during the onsite demo. Qualitative pain (manual processes, ERP-deferral risk) is clear, but moving to commercial
deltas.subject: Co-develop a quantified value case ahead of or during the onsite demo. Qualitative pain (manual processes, ERP-deferral risk) is clear, but moving to commercials requires a savings/ROI anchor. Work with Guy Elliott to model procurement volume, manual-touch cost, cycle-time reduction, and compliance-risk mitigation that Intake+S2C would deliver, so the demo lands with a business case the CFO can approve.

deltas.to: Unresolved deferral risk: buyer concern about waiting for ERP implementation before procurement tooling. If buyer decides ERP-first, deal slips indefinitely or
deltas.subject: Unresolved deferral risk: buyer concern about waiting for ERP implementation before procurement tooling. If buyer decides ERP-first, deal slips indefinitely or dies. Zycus positioning (low-integration quick win) is response, not resolution.
Philip Holliday
Kellie French
Ian Chilton

Multi-thread urgently: Only 1 buyer contact (Guy Elliott, mid-level Procurement lead) on a $200k S2P deal. Expand to senior stakeholders (Philip Holliday COO, Kellie French Corporate Services Manager, Ian Chilton IT lead) on the demo call to uncover Economic Buyer, validate urgency, and reduce single-thread risk. A deal this size cannot close through one Evaluator.

Convert the demo into a discovery + validation session: Use the onsite demo to surface quantified pain (current procurement cycle time, manual-process cost, error rate), map the decision process (RFI/RFP timeline, approval gates), identify competitors, and validate the low-integration/quick-value pitch with real buyer priorities. A demo without discovery leaves the MEDDPICC gaps unfilled.

Test and build the champion: Ask Guy Elliott on the demo call to bring his manager or the budget-holder into the next session, validate his level of urgency and influence, and assess whether he will advocate for Zycus internally. If he cannot or will not multi-thread us up, he is a gatekeeper, not a champion, and the deal is at risk.
